name: Living Canvas Product Explainer
description: Boutique-grade 16:9 SaaS launch/explainer built as ONE continuous living canvas: almost zero hard cuts, element-level pacing (a meaningful beat every 2.0-2.5s), a virtual camera that punches into persistent UI cards on the exact VO word, causal physics on every element (rank-sort shuffles, gravity rain, squash-stretch, transformation chains where scene B is born from scene A's matter), a 10-15s first-person story cold-open with emotional VO and a visual punchline, three-band music (mundane jazz bed -> hard-stop silence under the joke -> orchestral score at the thesis cut), a 30-60 event beat-locked SFX bus, and Sharp Tag data badges instead of template pills. Implemented in Remotion as a single flat actor timeline beat-locked to Whisper word timestamps, mixed in FFmpeg. Read LIVING_CANVAS_PLAYBOOK.md before any work: every duration, zoom factor, spring constant, and failure mode is specified there.
best_for: SaaS product launch videos and homepage explainers that must feel like a top boutique motion studio made them
positioning-driven launches where a story cold-open + humor earns the pitch (character VO -> punchline -> thesis slam)
feature walkthroughs told as camera journeys across persistent UI mockups (punch-ins synced to VO words)
16:9 masters for landing pages, YouTube, Product Hunt, with ad cutdowns derived later

stages.title: Reverse-engineer the target style at frame level
actions: Download 4-8 reference explainers (yt-dlp, <=1080p mp4)
Measure hard-cut statistics: ffmpeg select='gt(scene,0.22)' -> expect 0-3 cuts per 60s; the pace lives in element choreography, not editing
Coarse pass: 2fps 4x4 contact sheets to LOCATE the 3-6 most choreographed sequences per video
Dense pass: 8fps 6x4 sheets over each 4-10s sequence; reconstruct second-by-second timelines distinguishing ELEMENT motion vs CAMERA motion vs NEW LAYERS, with zoom-factor estimates and VO-word sync
Generalize into a numeric style bible (pacing table, camera state machine, FX catalog) BEFORE writing any code; skip this stage only if reusing the numbers already in LIVING_CANVAS_PLAYBOOK.md

stages.title: Script, story cold-open, and beat-locked VO
actions: Write the arc: story cold-open (10-15s, first person, emotional, visual punchline) -> dark-turn thesis line (THE hard cut) -> product reveal -> 3-4 feature chapters as clipped 2-second clauses -> payoff beat with one cheeky microcopy aside -> proof -> CTA with a typographic joke (strike-through)
Announcer VO: energetic TTS read of the pitch half; story VO: expressive TTS with emotion tags ([tired][excited][sighs]...) at stability ~0.35 style ~0.6

MANDATORY silence surgery on tagged reads (they render 40-60% dead air): silenceremove stop_duration=0.26 threshold=-38dB + atempo 1.08, then Whisper and CUT A WHOLE BEAT if the story still exceeds 15s (dropping the 'hope' beat so payoff follows action is usually funnier)
Whisper the final audio (word timestamps) and derive EVERY frame number from words: wordAt(t)=round(t*30). The audio drives the timeline; a new voice means re-locking everything
Keep the story voice intimate and the announcer confident; cap the loudness step at the handoff to ~7-8 dB (lift story VO ~+3 dB in the mix)

stages.title: Remotion single flat actor timeline
actions: One composition file: every element is an actor IIFE with enter/exit frames in a central T object; no scene wrappers, no Sequence grouping

Copy the motion helper library from the playbook verbatim: spr (d11/s210), actor, settle (micro-bounce on EVERYTHING that lands), slotAt (rank-sort shuffles), camRig (d16/s250 punches ~0.3s + 4%/s creep + velocity blur with 3.5px dead zone), shakeAt, deterministic rnd, typeOn

Per feature beat implement the S0-S7 camera state machine: oversized entrance settle -> hold with creep -> commit punch 1.3-1.4x centered on the sub-region the VO names -> creep-hold -> extraction punch 1.66-2.2x or zero-zoom pan -> FX burst with camera LOCKED -> accelerating exit ramp -> asymmetric whip-out

Give every beat ONE distinct causal mechanic from the catalog (tip-over fall, gravity rain, vacuum spiral, debris scatter, squash-stretch drop, pendulum swing, click thump, arc emission, cell drops, wire-draw, convergence beams, evidence-mounted claims, typographic strike) - never repeat one
Layout reflow: absolute-positioned rows at animated slot positions inside height-animated containers; width-push chips; yield when a second card claims space; chat push-up; the card physically GROWS
1-2 transformation chains max (gather -> flick -> orbit r205 -> collapse -> Flash+Ring+Sparks+shake -> next hero born 0.12->1 with brightness flare), 20s apart, on the two most important handoffs

Design system: Satoshi-class sans (900 headlines via local TTF + @remotion/fonts), Sharp Tag split-cell data badges (3px radius, mono value cell - NEVER glowing pills), ghost chapter titles at 4-6% opacity, 44px drifting grid + accent blobs, dark world with glowing horizon line, 3D-tilted power cards (perspective 1200-1400px, rotateX 12-16, rotateY 8-14, sin wobble) that UNTILT flat when the camera punches
Late story/section swaps: use the timeline-splice technique (frame = useCurrentFrame() - SHIFT) so downstream choreography keeps its numbers; gate replaced sections; run backgrounds on absolute frame

stages.title: Three-band music + SFX bus
actions: Generate the mundane bed (~12s elevator jazz: brushed drums, walking bass, noodling piano, naive office mood) and the score (cinematic orchestral, steady dynamics, no vocals) via text-to-music
Band structure: jazz 0 -> punchline-appear frame (hard-stop, 0.2s fade) -> SILENCE under the punchline read -> orchestral enters at the dark cut via adelay with 1s fade-in
NEVER sidechain-duck: flatten the score with acompressor (-24dB/4:1/25/450/makeup 3) + static EQ carve (1.8kHz -6dB, 350Hz -2dB) + constant volume 0.16; target music 10-11 dB under VO
Generate a 9-effect SFX kit via text-to-SFX (min 0.5s each): whooshes short/deep, click, typing, pop, glitch, riser, impact, chime

Map 30-60 beat-locked events (section whips 0.45, punch-ins 0.22 trimmed, shuffles 0.15, risers into slams, impacts on bursts, typing under every type-on, clicks, ~10 pops, chimes) and build the bus with a generated ffmpeg filter_complex_script (asplit per usage, atrim/afade/volume/adelay per instance, amix normalize=0)
Verify SFX placement with windowed volumedetect at event times vs silent windows

stages.title: Probe-QC loop, render, mix, verify
actions: Probe-still QC loop: render 10-20 single frames at every punch, shuffle mid-flight, FX peak, and transition; READ each image and fix before any full render

If the project lives in a cloud-synced folder, rsync to local tmp (exclude node_modules) and build there; render with --concurrency=4 --timeout=120000 (local fonts delayRender-timeout at concurrency 8)
Mix in FFmpeg with -c:v copy (4 stems: spliced VO, bed, score, sfx bus) -> loudnorm I=-16:TP=-1.5:LRA=11
Verify: duration, ebur128 I/LRA, volumedetect at splice points, proof frames extracted from the ACTUAL export

Run the ship-gate checklist in the playbook (cuts, holds, stagger, blur, mechanics uniqueness, reflow, cursor targets, accent discipline, LUFS); watch a 2fps contact sheet of your own render - no two adjacent frames may be identical

providers.voice: elevenlabs (expressive model with audio tags for story; standard model for announcer)

script_shape: Story cold-open (10-15s): first-person mundane action -> excitement -> deflation -> visual punchline read in silence
Dark turn (2-3s): THE hard cut; one brutal thesis line in kinetic type
Reveal (4-6s): logo squash-stretch drop, name types on, badge pendulum, accent underline draw
Features (3-4 chapters x 3-5s): one persistent card per chapter + camera journey + one unique causal mechanic
Payoff (4-6s): the consequence moment + one cheeky microcopy aside
Proof (3-4s): claims mounted on the evidence + one full-canvas word slam
CTA (5-7s): sequential end-card pops, strike-through joke, cursor click with particle ring, URL type-on, friction-killer microcopy
qc_checks: 0-3 hard cuts, each on a narrative pivot; none in the first 10s
no settled composition holds >3.5s; creep zoom on every hold; no frame fully static
every camera reframe lands on its VO word within +/-4 frames (or leads by 6-18)
every landing is staggered and settles with a micro-bounce; fast moves carry 1-3 frames of blur
one causal mechanic per beat, none repeated; reflow displaces - cards never overlap without yielding
cursor click targets computed from layout, verified in probe frames
story punchline reads in near-silence; music bands land on their exact frames
one accent phrase per headline; data badges are Sharp Tags, never glowing pills
fire/particle FX never cover the text they highlight
-16 LUFS, TP <= -1.5, music 10-11 dB under VO, VO handoff step <= 8 dB
deliverables: Remotion composition source + motion helper library (reusable)
style_bible.md when a fresh reference study was run
job_state.json with per-version changes and metrics
compliance_notes: Never name or imitate a specific studio's brand; the style is defined by the numeric grammar in LIVING_CANVAS_PLAYBOOK.md, not by copying any one company's assets
Third-party logos (search/AI engines, CMS platforms) may appear as real marks in app-icon tiles for factual integration claims; flag trademark review before paid ad placements
All UI shown is redrawn/stylized mockup content - never real customer data or fabricated competitor claims
